label: LBLJ_label
reason: On the lobby’s red stairs beside the pillar and 8‑star door, Mario performs rapid backward long jumps, then snaps upward along the wall and instantly appears under the floor (black void), indicating a hyperspeed clip used in the lobby BLJ to bypass the 8‑star door.

label: LBLJ_label
reason: In the castle lobby, Mario backs into the green pillar and repeats the crouched long‑jump animation in place, producing dust. He then snaps backward at extreme speed and the camera rushes toward the star door, followed by a sudden cut to the dark void with Mario still moving—evidence of clipping through the 8‑star door via a lobby BLJ.

label: LBLJ_label
reason: Mario performs repeated backwards long jumps against the lobby pillar/stair area, then bypasses the closed 8-star door without an opening animation. The camera instantly shows the small dark antechamber and wooden hallway behind the door, indicating a BLJ-based clip specific to the lobby (LBLJ).

label: Pole_glitch_label
reason: After a jump on the round platform, the camera cuts under an orange platform with a pole. Mario then appears instantly high on the pole, right at the socket/mesh area, with no climb animation or intermediate movement up the pole. This abrupt snap to the top of the pole indicates the pole warp behavior characteristic of Pole Glitch.

label: Normal
reason: Mario rises on a platform from a brick well and proceeds across moving platforms and up mesh ramps at normal speed. No abrupt teleports, hyperspeed buildup, geometry or hitbox clipping, or anomalous interactions are visible in the sequence.

label: Miscellaneous_wall_clipping_label
reason: Mario runs to the curved lobby wall, jumps, and then rises into the ceiling with his model intersecting and disappearing as the camera clips inside the geometry. There is no BLJ posture or visible speed buildup; the movement indicates bypassing collision via a wall/ceiling gap rather than hyperspeed.

label: LBLJ_label
reason: Mario performs rapid backwards long jumps up the lobby’s red-carpet stairs toward the 8-star door. His speed builds abnormally, the door-opening animation never occurs, and the camera clips through the star-plaque as Mario appears on the other side in the blue hallway. This is the lobby BLJ used to clip through the 8-star door.

label: BLJ_label
reason: On the endless staircase, Mario repeatedly long-jumps while facing the camera, gains abnormal backward speed, then suddenly rockets to the top hallway with the Bowser painting. The rapid acceleration and abrupt transition are characteristic of a standard BLJ; no sideways setup or location-specific variant is shown.
